title: abies douglasii var. fastigiata
intro: Abies douglasii var. fastigiata, also known as Douglas fir, is a coniferous evergreen tree that belongs to the Pinaceae family. It is native to the Pacific coast of North America, where it is valued for its fragrant timber, ornamental value, and medicinal properties. The essential oil extracted from the tree's needles and branches has been used to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. Herbal preparations of Douglas fir can be consumed as tea, taken in capsule form, or used in aromatherapy to promote relaxation and relieve stress.
